textDisplay: I have a feeling this film is going to be a *GREAT* installment in The Conjuring Universe, guys! The overall concept of Annabelle awakening all the evil in The Warren’s artifact room is brilliant. The plot, setting, actors, characters, premise, and scares look great, and it has that “Conjuring” feel we all love. textDisplay: The little kid can't die, because she's a young Judy. We see her in The Conjuring when she's around 7-9 years old, and again in Conjuring 2, when she's 14-16. Since you can see the cursed mirror thing that April has in the first Conjuring in this trailer and Judy obviously isn't 14 yet, It's safe to assume this movie takes place between those movies, and so Judy can't die. If you can't tell, I'm a huge nerd :))
textOriginal: The little kid can't die, because she's a young Judy. We see her in The Conjuring when she's around 7-9 years old, and again in Conjuring 2, when she's 14-16. Since you can see the cursed mirror thing that April has in the first Conjuring in this trailer and Judy obviously isn't 14 yet, It's safe to assume this movie takes place between those movies, and so Judy can't die. If you can't tell, I'm a huge nerd :))

textDisplay: Actually that is sorta but not really factual. The real Annabelle doll that the cinematic film Annabelle doll is based on is actually kept in Ed and Lorraine Warren’s house in a occult museum they ran out of there. The accident was also possibly an homage to the accident a motorcycle got in three hours after he tapped Annabelle’s case and made fun of her or to the “mysterious car troubles “ they had while transporting her.

textDisplay:
Who else thinks they need to make a horror movie where the main character is actually smart and just gets outsmarted by the demon but ends up thinking up a genius plan that actually makes sense, to beat the demon. Basically like a chess game in a horror movie. For example, the main character hears noises downstairs. In this situation the mc would take a safe route out of the house from their bedroom or upstairs. But the demon monster thing had already knew that the main character would try to exit from upstairs so he created the noises as a diversion BUT the real threat is outside. Before the Main character thought about going outisde, they decided to dump themselves in a tub of holy water. The demon had smelled the holy water and realized that it couldn’t touch the main character until the holy water wears out. Then the main character had already thought about the demon thingy creating a diversion so they decide to create a distraction to distract the demon doll thing guy so they can successfully escape then burn the house, burn their clothes, (with the holy water still on their body) and move to a whole other side of the Earth, the end.

BUT THEN The demon guy thing ends up acting like they died in the fire so they can silently trace the main character around. The main character had already thought this all through so he made a false plan about moving to one side of the Earth when in reality, they’re going to a church disguised as a airport to purify the demon with Jesus’s blood. The demon had already thought this would happen so he decided to keep his distance from the main character. BUT the main character had already thought that through so he had a special ops silent task force make a circle with holy water around  where the demon was following the main character so they could trap the demon. BUUUUUT the demon thingy fish guy had already though of that happening so he decided to give up and go back to the underworld (where he came from) before the task force completed their circle) (Once the demon goes back, he cant come back to Earth.) BUT if you think its done yet then you’re wrong, the demon had left a piece of his blood in a bottle of wine in a store. After the person who bought the bottle of wine and drunk the blood of the demon, the human would turn into another demon, which the NEW demon had made a ritual to summom back the OLD demon, and they both terrorize and kill the Earth, but just when humanity was about to be wiped out, GOD steps in and easily kills both of the demons which, leave 2 people on the Earth alive, and thats how Adam and Eve started.

(Yea I know, this is the most unrealistic thing ever and my grammar was total trash.)

textDisplay: The problem with the conjuring universe is the only person that seems to be able to make a good/great movie within it is James wan no matter how good the premise is. Insidious has been run into the ground and to make things even more wtf, it was ruined by the guy that writes all of them once wan let him take over directing duties. I know wan is extremely busy and sought after but all of this stuff is part of his legacy so I wish he would take a more hands on approach as a producer. He knows what works and even though he was using other people's scripts for the films he did he reworked them to be more effective. His ambitions more than likely expand way past horror but with his guiding hand I think he can do for horror what Kevin feige was able to do for the superhero genre and it would be one hell of a thing to do considering the potential profit margin for these kinds of movies. Several  hundred million dollars at the box office for films that usually max out at around 30-40 million dollars for their budget(with most of them not exceeding 15-20 million) is a lot more impressive than spending 350 mil (plus marketing costs that can get up to almost the same amount) to pull in a billion(+).
textOriginal: The problem with the conjuring universe is the only person that seems to be able to make a good/great movie within it is James wan no matter how good the premise is. Insidious has been run into the ground and to make things even more wtf, it was ruined by the guy that writes all of them once wan let him take over directing duties. I know wan is extremely busy and sought after but all of this stuff is part of his legacy so I wish he would take a more hands on approach as a producer. He knows what works and even though he was using other people's scripts for the films he did he reworked them to be more effective. His ambitions more than likely expand way past horror but with his guiding hand I think he can do for horror what Kevin feige was able to do for the superhero genre and it would be one hell of a thing to do considering the potential profit margin for these kinds of movies. Several  hundred million dollars at the box office for films that usually max out at around 30-40 million dollars for their budget(with most of them not exceeding 15-20 million) is a lot more impressive than spending 350 mil (plus marketing costs that can get up to almost the same amount) to pull in a billion(+).

textDisplay: Ok i want to preface this by saying i love creepy shit and hope spirits/ghosts are real but the Warrens are making it alot harder with their behavior these days. Think about this, they have a room IN THEIR HOUSE filled literally to the brim with "EXTREMELY DANGEROUS" cursed objects and yet they,for some reason, believe their young incredibly curious kid and or friends are going to simply never try to go in there? Its literally insane and utterly unrealistic ,no parent (even a bad one), if they knew these types of things were real ,would ever willingly choose to keep a cursed room with potentially deadly consequences in their young family home. Its just not believable, its in fact UN-believable!

2021 Annabelle Goes Back, Way Back.
Due to the strange and mysterious events surrounding the haunted doll, Annabelle, she finds out she has the ability to warp time, in essence, time travel. On her journey to travel back in time, she faces new challenges such as Chucky, Tiffany and other mofo dolls in Hollywood fighting for the spotlight. Then, she finds herself warped into a random black hole in space with all the horrible screenplays...forever. This isn’t your typical episode of The Twilight Zone, but Annabelle Goes Back..Wayyyyyy Baaaaack. Coming to theatres 2021 (flushing toilet noise 🚽)

textDisplay:
I’m excited for the movie, but the writing really hurts my brain cells.

They ask for two young girls to babysit at a house with the haunted room of all things evil (can the daughter not stay anywhere else like a friends house or the parents friends house?). They make the door easy to unlock instead of the Warrens keeping a key on them while they’re gone. One of the girls is a bit of a brat (never seen that character type in a horror movie before) and wants to go in the room even after being told specifically not to because it’s full of evil cursed objects. And even though Annabelle is contained in the glass box, the door to the room possibly lets her free bc who needs logic anymore. 

But we’re all going to see it anyways lol. It is what it is.
